acceptance_criteria.text: readOAuthToken(fsAccess) returns string or null (existing v4.7.0 semantics preserved)

proof.summary: Lines 67-100: readOAuthToken(fsAccess) exported. When fsAccess undefined, falls through to require('fs') (NFR-3 standalone mode). macOS keychain branch identical to statusline.js:130-151. File-based path uses fsr.readFileSync + getPath('claudeAiOauth.accessToken'). catch-all returns null. Semantics byte-identical to statusline.js v4.7.0 lines 128-161.
Case 1a (valid creds → token string), Case 2a (missing file → null), Case 3a (malformed JSON → null), Case 4a (missing accessToken → null) all verify the semantics.

acceptance_criteria.text: readCredentialsExpiresAt(fsAccess) exported (new helper, returns ms or null)

proof.summary: Lines 112-131: readCredentialsExpiresAt(fsAccess) exported. Reads claudeAiOauth.expiresAt from same credentials file. Returns number (ms-since-epoch) directly, or parses ISO-8601 string via Date.parse. Returns null on missing/unreadable/invalid.
Case 1b (numeric ms returned), Case 1c (ISO-8601 string parsed), Case 2b (missing file → null), Case 3b (malformed JSON → null), Case 4c (missing expiresAt field → null).

Lines 33-51: detectTermWidth() implementation is a 1:1 copy of statusline.js:803-819. Same tty require, same CONOUT$/tty device logic, same fd open/close, same destroy, same return 0 on catch. module.exports = { detectTermWidth } on line 53.

acceptance_criteria.text: No semantic change to either — existing v4.7.0 behaviour byte-identical

proof.summary: readOAuthToken: macOS branch identical to original; Win/Linux branch uses same fsr.readFileSync(credPath()) pattern with same catch → null. getPath logic is a local copy, same algorithm. Empty string accessToken returns null via || null (same as original). fsAccess=undefined falls through to fsBuiltin = require('fs') — exact original behaviour.
detectTermWidth body is identical line-for-line to statusline.js:804-819. No logic changes.
Returns 'line 128: function readOAuthToken()' — original still present at original line number; statusline.js unchanged.
Returns 'line 803: function detectTermWidth()' — original still present; statusline.js unchanged.

required_cases_covered.case_1_present_valid: case 1a (token), case 1b (expiresAt numeric), case 1c (expiresAt ISO string)
required_cases_covered.case_4_missing_access_token: case 4a (missing accessToken), case 4b (missing claudeAiOauth), case 4c (missing expiresAt)

extra_cases: extra: empty object in creds.json → both helpers return null without throwing
extra: claudeAiOauth.accessToken is empty string → readOAuthToken returns null (v4.7.0 || null preserved)
extra: readCredentialsExpiresAt with invalid ISO string → returns null

statusline_unchanged.note: Both functions remain at their original line numbers in statusline.js — no edits applied

no_new_deps.note: Only built-in modules used: child_process, path, fs, os, tty — all already in use by v4.7.0

detect-term-width.js: tty (lazy require inside function — same as original)

known_gaps: Test run exit code not directly verified via shell execution (task builder agent has no shell tool). All 13 test cases manually traced against creds.js implementation — each assertion maps to a verified code path.

detectTermWidth has no unit tests in this task — the function opens a TTY device which is headless-unfriendly. The function is an exact copy of the original; behaviour is verified by identity, not by new test execution. Test coverage for detectTermWidth deferred to integration tests in T7 where the full statusline is exercised.
